_comment: NEUTRAL FICTIONAL EXAMPLE (Loom 2.0-rc.8 WS3/WS10). Meridian Trust is an invented institution — it carries NO real policy, brand, regulatory interpretation or approval authority. An institution profile is how a repository's governed changes bind to the institution's approved BrainKit (institution/brainkit/): every change must conform to institutional identity, terminology, architecture principles and technology policy, be approved by the institutional-context-owner, and carry brainkit-provenance evidence. Institution profiles compose ON TOP OF base + jurisdiction + product profiles — they only ever ADD requirements, never loosen them. Once an envelope names this profile in required_profiles, the brainkit-conformance gate and brainkit-provenance evidence are mandatory-when-compiled (they apply at the low tier, so they cover every tier). A generic repository that names no institution profile is unaffected. `brainkit.path` is the pinned BrainKit this profile binds; the policy compiler folds the live BrainKit package digest into the plan's profile binding, so a BrainKit revision makes a stored plan stale and forces recompilation. This is a public example: real institutional profiles will normally live in a private repository.
